shortlist_full.thesis: Oracle infra leader emerging from a long base with a concrete TradFi catalyst and a volume_rank jump (257->78); the largest May unlock is already digested, removing the main overhang. Enter on confirmed structure above the recent demand zone, scale in, keep size modest given risk-off tape.
shortlist_full.fundamentals: Pyth is a first-party oracle network delivering 380+ low-latency price feeds across 40-100+ chains, with publishers like Binance, Jane Street, Cboe and OKX; the PYTH token governs the network and underpins its data economy. Why now: it pivoted from pure DeFi into a wholesale TradFi data layer (Data Marketplace backed by Fidelity, Euronext, Tradeweb) and powered ~$130B volume in May (~$110B RWA perps), plus monthly PYTH buybacks via the Reserve. Mid-cap (~$250-280M) with deep liquidity across 95+ exchanges.
shortlist_full.catalyst: Data Marketplace launch with major TradFi institutions and US Commerce on-chain macro data distribution, plus rising RWA-perps volume flowing through Pyth feeds and ongoing PYTH buybacks.

signals.google_trends: Search and media interest rising around on-chain GDP/RWA oracle narrative; PYTH cited as institutional-infra breakout pick.
signals.social: Community sentiment flagged bullish on CoinGecko; recurring coverage of institutional partnerships and buyback mechanism.
signals.binance: volume_rank jumped 257->78 this cycle; scanner stage 2; Binance is a Pyth data publisher and lists PYTH/USDT with healthy depth.
signals.onchain: ~$130B total volume in May (~$110B RWA perps) and ~$100B already in June; ~7.88B circulating of 10B supply with the big cliff unlock now cleared.

shortlist_full.risk: Remaining vesting/unlock supply (2.1B+ PYTH locked) can cap rallies; oracle competition from Chainlink; in a risk-off tape mid-cap infra alts can bleed regardless of fundamentals.

shortlist_full.thesis: Established DeFi yield protocol transitioning Stage 1->2 after rebounding ~90% off the $0.97 base to ~$1.85, with a volume_rank jump (172->62) and scanner stage 2 confirmation. Structure constructive while it holds above $1.60; enter on support holds, invalidate below $1.32.
shortlist_full.fundamentals: Pendle is a yield-tokenization protocol splitting yield-bearing assets into Principal (PT) and Yield (YT) tokens via a time-decay AMM, with a market for fixed/leveraged yield; PENDLE (now sPENDLE) captures fees and governs incentive distribution. Why now: it is riding the RWA/stablecoin-yield meta (new regulated-stablecoin pools, institutional mEVUSD strategy) and executed a tokenomics overhaul (vePENDLE->sPENDLE with buybacks and lower emissions). Mid-cap (~$250M) with TVL historically >$4B and liquidity across 100+ exchanges.
shortlist_full.catalyst: sPENDLE tokenomics upgrade (buybacks + reduced emissions) plus new RWA/stablecoin yield pool integrations (USDG, apxUSD, institutional EU products) expanding fee demand.

signals.google_trends: Rising interest tied to the stablecoin-yield/RWA narrative where Pendle is repeatedly featured as a core platform.
signals.social: CoinGecko community bullish; coverage frames Pendle as a maturing, fee-generating protocol with buyback support.
signals.binance: volume_rank jumped 172->62; scanner stage 2; PENDLE/USDT is the most active pair on Binance with solid depth.
signals.onchain: Recovered from $0.97 to ~$1.85 (+90%); 15/30 green days last month; TVL historically above $4B; sPENDLE buybacks reduce float.

shortlist_full.risk: Weekly timeframe still shows a falling 200-day MA (longer-term overhang); loss of $1.60 then $1.32 support would break the thesis; DeFi-wide risk-off could stall yield demand.

shortlist_full.thesis: DePIN/AI-storage L1 in a multi-month accumulation/compression structure with a volume_rank jump (243->85); a high-conviction infra name coiling for a Stage 1->2 transition. This is the most speculative of the three: only add on a confirmed reclaim of $2.20-2.50 with volume, keep size small.
shortlist_full.fundamentals: Arweave provides permanent, one-time-fee decentralized storage (the Permaweb); the AR token pays for storage and is progressively locked into the Storage Endowment as usage grows, while the AO compute layer (live since Feb 2025) adds a direct utility/demand link and an AI-data-provenance narrative. Why now: AO maturation plus AI/DePIN sector rotation, with AR outperforming BTC over the prior month. Smaller mid-cap (~$120M) — liquid on Binance but thinner than PYTH/PENDLE, so size accordingly.
shortlist_full.catalyst: AO compute adoption ramp (developer activity, persistent processes) and AI-data-provenance demand driving storage usage that locks AR into the endowment; sector capital rotating into AI/DePIN infra.

signals.google_trends: Permanent-storage/AI-data narrative interest steady-to-rising; AR anchored in Web3 infrastructure conversations.
signals.social: Core community sentiment optimistic per surveys; recurring AO/Permaweb ecosystem updates.
signals.binance: volume_rank jumped 243->85 this cycle; AR/USDT liquid on Binance, the most active venue.
signals.onchain: Trading in a compressed descending channel near $1.80 with neutral (not bearish) momentum; AR outperformed BTC ~10.7% over the prior month; fixed supply with endowment lockup.

shortlist_full.risk: No fresh hard catalyst this month and momentum still only neutral — breakout unconfirmed; competition from Filecoin and other storage/DePIN; smallest cap of the three means higher volatility and thinner liquidity in a risk-off tape; failure to hold ~$1.00-1.50 opens deeper downside.
